description.p: You replace your wizard’s spellbook with tattoos etched upon your body. These living etchings serve as your grimoire. When you prepare wizard spells, the tattoos on your body shift and realign, glowing faintly with arcane light.

When you find a spell, you can inscribe it onto your skin by spending 2 hours and 25 gp per level of the spell. If your tattoos are ever destroyed, you can recreate them during a long rest at the same cost and time as replacing a lost spellbook.

sheet.description: Inscribing a spell onto your body as a tattoo costs 2 hours and 25 gp per spell level. Destroyed tattoos can be recreated during a long rest at the same cost and time as a lost spellbook.

description.p: You gain the ability to inscribe a magical tattoo onto one willing creature other than yourself at the end of a long rest. The tattoo lasts until you finish your next long rest, or until its Script is expended. A creature can't benefit from more than one Shared Ink tattoo at a time:

ul.li: Ward of Ink. The creature gains a +1 bonus to AC.
Strike of Ink. Once per turn, when the creature hits with an attack, it deals extra damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

Script of Ink. Choose a spell from your grimoire with an instantaneous duration. The creature can cast that spell once without expending a spell slot, using your spell save DC and spell attack bonus. Once the spell is cast, the tattoo and all its effects end immediately.

sheet.description: At the end of a long rest, inscribe a magical tattoo on a willing creature. The tattoo lasts until your next long rest or until its Script is expended. A creature can't benefit from more than one Shared Ink tattoo at a time. Ward of Ink: +1 AC. Strike of Ink: +Int mod damage on attacks (once per turn). Script of Ink: Cast a chosen instantaneous spell once using your spellcasting ability.

description.p: you gain further control of the ink in your body, allowing you to slip away when danger strikes. When you take damage from a creature you can see, you can use your reaction to teleport up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

When you use Ink Step, ink lashes out at the creature that damaged you. That creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or be blinded until the start of its next turn.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

sheet.description: When you take damage from a creature you can see, you can teleport up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space. The creature that damaged you must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or be blinded until the start of its next turn. You can use this feature {{ink step:usage}} times, and you regain all uses on a long rest.

description.p: When you take damage while concentrating on a spell, your tattoos flare to life, defending your magic. You can use your reaction to automatically succeed on the Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration.

In addition, inky tendrils lash out from your tattoos. All creatures of your choice in a 10-foot emanation must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 2d6 force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest or cast a spell of 3rd level or higher that requires concentration.

sheet.description: When you take damage while concentrating on a spell, you can use your reaction to automatically succeed on your Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration. Inky tendrils lash out from your tattoos: all creatures of your choice in a 10-foot emanation must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 2d6 force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest or cast a spell of 3rd level or higher that requires concentration.

p.em: 14th-level Order of the Living Grimoire feature

description.p: YYour living tattoos resonate with the flow of your spells, spreading their power to others in the heat of battle. Whenever you cast a spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you and immediately inscribe them with a Shared Ink tattoo. This tattoo lasts until the start of your next turn.

If the spell you cast has an instantaneous duration and is from your grimoire, the Script of Ink effect is automatically loaded with that spell, and the creature can cast it once without expending a spell slot, using your spell save DC and spell attack bonus. Once the spell is cast, the tattoo and all its effects end immediately.

sheet.description: When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher, choose one creature within 30 ft. and inscribe them with a Shared Ink tattoo that lasts until the start of your next turn. If the spell is instantaneous and from your grimoire, the Script of Ink is loaded with that spell.
